返回本批次

乔治·戈布尔去世

George Goble has died

www.legacy.com 作者 finaard 分数 115 评论 21 2026/04/03 02:21
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 来自印第安纳州西拉斐特的乔治·哈里·戈布尔（George Harry Goble），被亲切地称为“GHG”，于 2026 年 3 月 18 日在拉斐特 Majestic Care 平静地去世。现年 73 岁。乔治于 1952 年 12 月 11 日出生于印第安纳州森特维尔，在那里度过了他的早年时光，然后开始追求他对技术的热情。在他去世之前，他的父母乔治·华盛顿·戈布尔（George Washington Goble）和露西尔·塞雷皮纳斯·戈布尔（Lucille Serepinas Goble）以及他的妹妹卡罗尔·安·戈布尔·沃纳（Carol Ann Goble Warner）也先于他去世。他的侄子，纽约市的科迪·华纳；乔治还留下了许多珍贵的朋友，包括凯西·威洛伍德 (Kathy Willowood)、迈克尔·帕金森 (Michael Parkinson) 和伊冯·帕金森 (Yvonne Parkinson)、约翰·菲利普斯 (John Phillips)、乔·西乔斯 (Joe Cychosz)、玛吉 (Maggie) 和埃利奥特·戴尔 (Elliot Dale)、雷克斯·邦特拉格 (Rex Bontrager)、黛安·S (Diane S)、南希·布尔格 (Nancy Bulger)、吉姆·莱纳特 (Jim Lehnert)、戴夫·蔡西 (Dave Chasey) 和丹·圣约翰 (Dan St. John)，以及几个表兄弟：金 (Kim)、黛比 (Debbie)、米奇 (Mikey)、 Buzzy、Sara 和 Susie.George 毕业于普渡大学，获得电气工程理学学士学位。他成为普渡大学工程计算机网络 (ECN) 的创始成员，并在那里奉献了自己的职业生涯直至退休。作为计算领域真正的先驱，他是 UNIX 操作系统的早期专家，并与 Dennis Ritchie、Ken Thompson 和 Bill Joy 等行业传奇人物合作。为了表彰他的贡献，普渡大学于 2022 年授予他杰出电气和计算机工程研究员的称号。他的创新包括通过连接 DEC VAX 系统进行并行处理、开发 PNET（一种早期网络系统）以及广播第一个基于网络的日食视频。前瞻性思维发生在几年前，发生在英尺的海滩上。佛罗里达州劳德代尔堡，乔治坐在沙滩上，拿着通过手机连接的笔记本电脑，成为普渡大学第一批通过移动连接到电脑上阅读电子邮件的人之一。正如一位朋友所说，“这确实是一段历史……当时苹果有一整个工程团队试图做到这一点，而乔治在海滩上实现了这一目标。”除了计算之外，乔治还对制冷技术做出了贡献，包括在逐步淘汰消耗臭氧层制冷剂期间与 R-406A 相关的工作，以及后来旨在提高热力学效率的创新。2006 年，他与杰里·伍德尔 (Jerry Woodall) 合作开发了一种氢燃料系统，使用固体铝将水分解为氢气和乔治在一年一度的 Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) 野餐会上，用木炭和液氧在创纪录的三秒内准备好了烧烤架，其令人难忘的个性和幽默引起了全国的关注。 1995 年 6 月，戴夫·巴里 (Dave Barry) 在一篇专栏中报道了这一壮举，并为他赢得了 1996 年搞笑诺贝尔化学奖。 在他的个人生活中，乔治是一个循规蹈矩、享受简单乐趣的人。他是西拉斐特当地必胜客的熟面孔，多年来每天都在 Noble Roman's 与朋友一起享用午餐。他那辆亮黄色的悍马车成了当地的传奇人物，无论天气如何，总能找到停车位。过去六年来照顾乔治的詹妮弗·N·伊斯贝尔(Jennifer N. Isbell)第一次见到他是在西拉斐特的太阳黑子自然市场当店员时。最初在店里的友好交谈后来发展成了深厚而持久的友谊。随着时间的推移，詹妮弗成为了他忠实的照顾者，他们的关系远远超出了照顾的范畴。通过日常生活、对话和安静的时刻，他们的关系发展成为一种深深的信任、尊重和陪伴。詹妮弗坚定不移的同情心和奉献精神使乔治在晚年能够保持舒适、支持和熟悉的氛围。她的出现不仅给他带来了关怀，还给他带来了欢笑和尊严——这对他来说意味着整个世界。乔治将被铭记为一位杰出的创新者、一位忠诚的朋友和一位独一无二的人，他的智慧、创造力和幽默给所有认识他的人留下了持久的印象。目前没有安排任何服务。通过 www.hippensteelfuneralservice.com 在线分享回忆和哀悼，WLFI 于 2026 年 3 月 31 日在 Legacy.com 上发布讣告。

Original Summary

  1. George Harry Goble, affectionately known as "GHG," of West Lafayette, Indiana, passed away peacefully on March 18, 2026, at Majestic Care of Lafayette. He was 73 years old.Born on December 11, 1952, in Centerville, Indiana, George spent his early years there before pursuing his passion for technology. He was preceded in death by his parents, George Washington Goble and Lucille Serepinas Goble, and by his sister, Carol Ann Goble Warner.He is survived by his brother-in-law, Michael Warner, with whom he remained close; his nephew, Cody Warner of New York City; and his devoted friend and caregiver, Jennifer N. Isbell of West Lafayette.George also leaves behind many cherished friends, including Kathy Willowood, Michael and Yvonne Parkinson, John Phillips, Joe Cychosz, Maggie and Elliot Dale, Rex Bontrager, Diane S, Nancy Bulger, Jim Lehnert, Dave Chasey, and Dan St. John, as well as several cousins: Kim, Debbie, Mikey, Buzzy, Sara, and Susie.George graduated from Purdue University with a Bachelor of Science degree in Electrical Engineering. He became a founding member of the Purdue Engineering Computer Network (ECN), where he dedicated his career until retirement. A true pioneer in computing, he was an early expert in the UNIX operating system and collaborated with industry legends such as Dennis Ritchie, Ken Thompson, and Bill Joy.In recognition of his contributions, Purdue University honored him in 2022 as an Outstanding Electrical and Computer Engineering Fellow.His innovations included early work in parallel processing by connecting DEC VAX systems, developing PNET (an early networking system), and broadcasting one of the first web-based videos of a solar eclipse.A striking example of his forward-thinking occurred years ago on a beach in Ft. Lauderdale, Florida, where George-sitting on the sand with a laptop connected via his cell phone-became one of the first people to read email over a mobile connection to a computer at Purdue. As a friend noted, "This was a real bit of history… At the time Apple had a whole engineering team trying to do this and here's George on the beach making it happen."Beyond computing, George contributed to refrigeration technology, including work related to R-406A during the phase-out of ozone-depleting refrigerants, and later innovations aimed at improving thermodynamic efficiency.In 2006, he partnered with Jerry Woodall to develop a hydrogen fuel system that used solid aluminum to split water into hydrogen and aluminum oxide, successfully demonstrated at Purdue.George's unforgettable personality and humor gained national attention when he prepared a barbecue grill in a record three seconds using charcoal and liquid oxygen at the annual Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) picnic. The feat was featured in a June 1995 column by Dave Barry and earned him the 1996 Ig Nobel Prize in Chemistry.In his personal life, George was a man of routine and simple pleasures. He was a familiar face at the local Pizza Hut in West Lafayette and enjoyed daily lunches with friends at Noble Roman's for many years. His bright yellow Hummer became something of a local legend, always managing to find a parking spot no matter the weather.Jennifer N. Isbell, who cared for George over the past six years, first met him when she was working as a clerk at the Sunspot Natural Market in West Lafayette. What began as friendly conversations at the store grew into a deep and lasting friendship. Over time, Jennifer became his devoted caregiver, and their bond extended far beyond caregiving. Through daily routines, conversations, and quiet moments, their relationship grew into one of deep trust, respect, and companionship. Jennifer's unwavering compassion and dedication allowed George to remain comfortable, supported, and surrounded by familiarity in his later years. Her presence brought him not only care, but laughter and dignity-gifts that meant the world to him.George will be remembered as a brilliant innovator, a loyal friend, and a one-of-a-kind individual whose intellect, creativity, and humor left a lasting impression on all who knew him.No services are scheduled at this time. Share memories and condolences online at www.hippensteelfuneralservice.comObituary published on Legacy.com by WLFI on Mar. 31, 2026.

评论（默认中文）

finaard 2026/04/03 02:21
他的讣告或维基百科页面非常值得一读，了解他所参与的事情 - 尽管他最出名的可能是使用液氧在 5 秒内点燃烧烤炉，并因此给当地消防部门带来麻烦。

他曾经在他的网站上播放过该视频 - 我是在该视频发生后不久通过新闻组讨论发现的。这是我通过网络浏览器下载的第一批视频之一，几乎可以肯定也是我见过的第一部用数码相机制作的视频。

His obituary or wikipedia page are well worth a read for what he was involved in - though he probably is best known for lighting a BBQ in under 5 seconds by use of liquid oxygen, and getting into trouble with the local firedepartment for that.

He used to have that video on his website - which I've discovered via a Usenet discussion not too long after it happened. It was one of the first videos I've downloaded via a web browser, and almost certainly the first video made with a digital camera I've ever seen.

在 HN 中查看
sponaugle 2026/04/03 02:59
George 非常喜欢视频方面的东西 - 他的办公室里有成堆的 8 毫米录像带，当然还有成堆的 EB 驱动器。他有许多不同的相机，并且总是尝试新的相机。 他也是激光光盘的早期采用者，我毕业时有几张他送给我的光盘。
George was really into video stuff - he had stacks of 8mm video tapes in his office, and of course stacks of exabyte drives. He had many different cameras and was always trying new ones out. He was also a really early adopter of laserdiscs, and I have a few discs he gave me when I graduated.
在 HN 中查看
throwup238 2026/04/03 03:33
有没有可能将所有这些内容发送给互联网档案馆或档案团队？
Any chance all of that will be sent to the Internet Archive or Archive Team?
在 HN 中查看
svggrfgovgf 2026/04/03 05:51
很抱歉听到。我记得乔治在普渡大学将 EE PDP-11 作为分时系统与 Lear Siegler ADM-3A 终端一起运行，而 CS 系（作为理学院的一部分）正在使用打孔卡！ EE 部门有一个房间，大约有 10 台 ADM-3A，连接到一台具有 100KB RAM（512KB？）的 PDP-11。

（ADM-3A 的照片位于 http://dunfield.classiccmp.org/altair/altair5.htm 它有24行80个字符，黑底绿字，加上一些可以用来画图的图形字符。）

Sorry to hear. I remember George running the EE PDP-11 as a time share system with Lear Siegler ADM-3A terminals at Purdue while the CS department (as part of the school of science) was using punch cards! The EE department had a room with 10 or so ADM-3As connected to a single PDP-11 that had a few 100KBs of RAM (512KB?).

(photos of ADM-3A at http://dunfield.classiccmp.org/altair/altair5.htm It has 24 lines of 80 characters with green text on black background plus some graphics characters which could be used to draw pictures.)

在 HN 中查看
nullbyte808 2026/04/03 03:58
“他的前瞻性思维的一个引人注目的例子发生在几年前佛罗里达州劳德代尔堡的海滩上，乔治坐在沙滩上，拿着通过手机连接的笔记本电脑，成为普渡大学第一批通过移动连接到计算机上阅读电子邮件的人之一。正如一位朋友所说，“这确实是一段历史......当时苹果有一整个工程团队试图做到这一点，而乔治在海滩上实现了这一目标。”

惊人的。安息吧。

"A striking example of his forward-thinking occurred years ago on a beach in Ft. Lauderdale, Florida, where George-sitting on the sand with a laptop connected via his cell phone-became one of the first people to read email over a mobile connection to a computer at Purdue. As a friend noted, "This was a real bit of history… At the time Apple had a whole engineering team trying to do this and here's George on the beach making it happen.""

Amazing. RIP.

finaard 2026/04/03 04:12
他的旧网站的存档副本有一张海滩的照片： https://web.archive.org/web/20000511210957/http://ghg.ecn.pu...

这是主要用于烧烤照明的页面： https://web.archive.org/web/20000511170940/http://ghg.ecn.pu...

The archived copy of his old website has a picture from the beach: https://web.archive.org/web/20000511210957/http://ghg.ecn.pu...

That's the page mostly dedicated to BBQ lighting: https://web.archive.org/web/20000511170940/http://ghg.ecn.pu...

tantalor 2026/04/03 02:34

Lighting a charcoal grill with liquid oxygen: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UjPxDOEdsX8

sponaugle 2026/04/03 02:44
我拿着相机拍摄其中一些视频。 真是一段美好的时光！
I was holding the camera for some of these videos. Such a great time!
在 HN 中查看
gorgoiler 2026/04/03 03:32
这让我想起我曾经读过的一篇评论，说外星访客到达地球后，会震惊地看到我们如何在含 20% 氧气的巨大气态海洋底部生活。

我们周围几乎所有重要的人和事只要发生一次事故，就会消失在炽热、乌黑的火焰中。

It reminds me of a comment I once read about how alien visitors, upon arriving on Earth, would be appalled to see how we live our lives at the bottom of a giant gaseous ocean of 20% oxygen.

Almost everyone and everything around us of any importance is one mishap away from going disappearing in a hot, sooty flame.

在 HN 中查看
dekhn 2026/04/03 03:46
有关此问题的另一个观点，请参阅《Shroud》一书（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shroud_(Tchaikovsky_novel)），有各种与你的观点相关的关于氧气的俏皮评论。

（总的来说，这是一本很棒的书，但是关于我们在生活环境中使用挥发性气体的部分非常简洁）

For another perspective on this, see the book Shroud (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shroud_(Tchaikovsky_novel)), there is all sorts of nifty commentary on oxygen related to your point.

(it's a great book in general, but the bit about our use of a volatile gas for a living environment is pretty neat)

在 HN 中查看
sponaugle 2026/04/03 02:43
听到很难过！ 我在普渡大学本科期间一直为乔治工作。 他是一位了不起的老板，对 Unix 的一切充满热情。 有一段时间，他的小型货车上挂着 UNIX 牌照。
Sad to hear! I worked for George for all of my undergraduate time at Purdue. He was an amazing boss with such a passion for all things unix. For a while he had the UNIX license plate on his minivan.
在 HN 中查看
housel 2026/04/03 06:34
从 1988 年到 1990 年，我也为他工作。当我帮助系统管理大学正在测试的各种 BSD 机器（CCI Tahoe 和 Gould NP-1）时，他为我提供了指导，并监督我修复 Berkeley Pascal 编译器中的错误的工作。 看着他在开车时将他的早期型号摩托罗拉手机置于服务模式并调整寄存器值，这很有趣。当然，我很高兴在各种奇怪的时间找到他在他的办公室，听他咆哮各种技术话题。
I worked for him as well, from 1988 through 1990. He mentored me as I helped sysadmin various BSD machines the university was beta-testing (CCI Tahoe and Gould NP-1), and supervised my work fixing bugs in the Berkeley Pascal compiler. It was fun watching him put his early-model Motorola cell phone into service mode and tweak register values... while he was driving. And of course I enjoyed finding him in his office at all sorts of weird hours and listening to him rant about various technical topics.
在 HN 中查看
sponaugle 2026/04/03 09:56
那太棒了。 NP-1 很棒。 我花了很多时间在 en.ecn.purdue.edu 上工作 - 一些磁带驱动程序、一些维护和许多软件项目 - 那时候每个人都在同一台机器上从终端工作，这真的很酷。 美好时光！
That is awesome. The NP-1s were great. I spent lots of time working on en.ecn.purdue.edu - Some tape drivers, some maintenance, and lots of software projects - it was really cool that in those days everyone was on the same machine, working from terminals. Good times!
在 HN 中查看
runjake 2026/04/03 03:42
给各位年轻人： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_H._Goble
For you youngsters: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_H._Goble
在 HN 中查看
thesuperbigfrog 2026/04/03 03:35
乔治的个人主页（好像是一面镜子： https://www.bkinzel.de/misc/ghg/index.html）以及烧烤照明视频和 TWINKIES 实验（原始站点已消失，但已存档： https://web.archive.org/web/20060101093459/http://www.twinki...）是 90 年代末令人惊叹的网站。
George's personal home page (seems to be a mirror: https://www.bkinzel.de/misc/ghg/index.html) with the grill lighting video and the TWINKIES experiments (original site gone, but archived: https://web.archive.org/web/20060101093459/http://www.twinki...) were amazing web sites in the late 90s.
在 HN 中查看
davidbjaffe 2026/04/03 04:18
当我上初中和高中时，我会去普渡大学的国际象棋俱乐部。 他是一个经常开怀大笑、有趣的人。 我们会下双速国际象棋（我们称之为“p'dorky”）和其他愚蠢的事情。 我不知道他继续做了他所做的很酷的事情。
When I was in junior high school and high school, I would hang out at the Purdue University chess club. He was a regular, prone to laughter, a funny guy. We would play double speed chess (which we called "p'dorky") and other silliness. I had no idea he went on to do the cool things that he did.
在 HN 中查看
tartoran 2026/04/03 09:44
安息吧
RIP
在 HN 中查看
dublin 2026/04/03 08:22
几年前，我联系了他，希望用他的 R-12 来替代我的 1975 年旧法拉利，而不是改装它。 它运作完美 - 更好的 甚至比Freon-12还要多。 这是美国环保局拒绝广泛使用它的唯一原因。 他的网站（ghgcool，IIRC，我确信现在已经消失了）告诉我，你也可以混合丁烷和异丙烷作为 R-12 的优质替代品，但他没有采用这种方法，因为他知道 EPA 会出于安全原因取消它 - 尽管它只比 R-12 稍微更易燃，但混合了所需的压缩机油。

乔治是一个非常有趣的人，一个真正的黑客中的黑客，我真的很喜欢和他交谈。

I contacted him a number of years ago about his R-12 replacement for my old 1975 Ferrari, rather than converting it. It worked perfectly - better than Freon-12, even. Which is the only reason the EPA refused to allow it to be widely used. His web site (ghgcool, IIRC, I'm sure long gone by now) taught me that you can also mix butane and isopropane as a superior drop-in substitute for R-12, but he didn't pursue that approach because he knew that the EPA would kill it on safety grounds - even though it was only slightly more flammable than R-12 with the required compressor oil mixed into it.

George was a really interesting guy, a true hacker's hacker, and I truly enjoyed talking with him.

在 HN 中查看
anthk 2026/04/03 05:25
Pentium4/Athlon 下的双 CPU Unix 也可以用作烧烤炉。

另外，还有一台没有风扇的 G5 PPC Mac。

A dual CPU Unix under a Pentium4/Athlon would work as a BBQ too.

Also, a G5 PPC Mac without fans.

SirFatty 2026/04/03 02:47
他在《鸟儿与蜜蜂》中表现出色。
He was great in "The Birds and the Bees".
在 HN 中查看
svggrfgovgf 2026/04/03 05:56
乔治·戈布尔错了——这是计算机科学家而不是演员。
Wrong George Goble - this is the computer scientist not the actor.
在 HN 中查看
